rulings.Case Title: ATHECIA SANCHEZ VS. SAN FRANCISCO POLICE DEPARTMENT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion (Opening Brief) For Writ Of Mandate To Enforce The California Public Records Act
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, April 17, 2026, Line 1. Petitioner Athecia Sanchez's motion for a writ of mandate to enforce the California Public Records Act ("CPRA") is granted. No later than May 8, 2026 respondent San Francisco Police Department must produce to petitioner all responsive documents sought by petitioner that it has not previously produced without any redactions except those documents claimed to be privileged by the attorney-client privilege or work product doctrine. For any document withheld on the grounds of privilege, respondent shall prepare a privilege log which must be provided to petitioner no later than May 8, 2026. Per Government Code 7922.600(a), respondent is required to assist petitioner in obtaining the documents sought by petitioner. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, April 17, 2026, Line 2. 2 - Plaintiffs' motion to compel further responses to document requests 42-47 from defendant Abbott Laboratories is granted in full. rulings.Case Title: D&M CARRIERS, LLC DBA FREYMILLER VS. PROGRESSIVE INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, April 17, 2026, Line 4. 1 - DEFENDANT PROGRESSIVE CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant Progressive Casualty Insurance Company's demurrer to the sole cause of action for bad faith insurance practice in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiff D&M Carriers LLC is sustained without leave to amend. Insurance Code 790.03, the sole legal basis for D&M's sole claim, does not create a private right of action. Because D&M has not shown that there is a reasonable possibility that it can allege a viable claim against Progressive, D&M's request for leave to amend is denied. D&M's apparent acknowledgment that Progressive did not issue a policy covering the November 30, 2023 Oklahoma City incident shows that D&M will not be able to allege a viable claim against Progressive. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DEBORAH COOK VS. DAVID RONALD SCHOOP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Requests For Admission To Plaintiff Deborah Cook, Set One Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, April 17, 2026, Line 5. Defendant David Schoop's motion to deem admitted set one requests for admission and for monetary sanctions is granted in large part. Schoop's set one requests for admission are deemed admitted due to plaintiff Deborah Cook's failure to serve responses to that discovery. Cook must pay reasonable monetary sanctions of $510 no later than May 12, 2026. No opposition filed. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: XIAO ZHU YU VS. BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT DISTRICT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, April 17, 2026, Line 6. Defendant San Francisco Bay Area Rapid Transit District's demurrer to the sole cause of action for negligence alleged in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Xiao Yu is sustained with leave to amend for the final opportunity to allege a viable claim against BART. Plaintiff fails to allege any ultimate facts supporting an exception to the general rule that public entities do not owe any duty to protect individuals from criminal conduct committed by third parties. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: MARTHE SCHREIBER VS. BRENT & FIOL, LLP
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Friday, April 17, 2026, Line 7. Defendant Brent & Fiol's demurrer to both causes of action in the complaint filed by Marthe Schreiber is sustained without leave to amend. The face of the complaint and judicially noticeable documents show that both causes of action are time-barred and that there is no reasonable possibility that Schreiber would be able to amend to allege any claims that are not time-barred. No opposition filed. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom.
